title: kube play: add exit-code propagation
body:
PR to implement https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RUN-1776. Please see the individual commits for details.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Add a new `ExitCode` field to Quadlet `.kube` files to allow for reflecting the exit codes of failed containers.
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Still WIP but in an advanced stage and ready to review and play with it.  Tests are there but I still need to go over the docs.

@ygalblum PTAL

This integration also brings up the question whether Quadlet should force `kube play` to default to a `Restart=Never` for the containers.  Currently it's `always` which is compatible with K8s but I see little value when running in systemd.

Not needed in this PR but something to consider before Quadlet goes GA.

> I'm not sure what I think about not having a simple argument for kube play.

Are you referring to the "trick" of using an annotation?  I figured it's cleaner to not expose a flag as it's not something users should use directly but only via Quadlet.
Ready for another/final round of reviews :checkered_flag:

> > I'm not sure what I think about not having a simple argument for kube play.
> 
> Are you referring to the "trick" of using an annotation? I figured it's cleaner to not expose a flag as it's not something users should use directly but only via Quadlet.

Yes, that's what I meant. It's a nice trick for sure. But, now, it means that users can pass it in the YAML file. So, it brings up new issues:
1. You need to test conflicts between the YAML and the argument
2. What about `kube generate` should it include the annotation now?

OK, all comments should be addressed now:
* Moved from an annotation to a proper field in the container config
* Added a new hidden --service-exit-code-propagation flag
* Fixed the formatting issue in the docs

@TomSweeneyRedHat @edsantiago @ygalblum PTAL

FYI: Starting tomorrow, I'll be OOO but can continue once I am back.
@vrothberg in case you haven't seen it, CI is failing, man-page inconsistency
